1|i0herf: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM h3. *Description:*

When a *FINAL SUN email* is triggered, the *Client Start Up Time* in the email displays a different format than the *Client Start Up Time* shown on the *Provider Network Contract (PNC)* record in Salesforce.
The time value should match exactly between the email and the Salesforce record.

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Log in to *Salesforce QA Sandbox*.
# Navigate to the *Provider Network Contract (PNC)* record.
# Note the *Client Start Up Time* (e.g., {{12:15:00 AM}}).
# Trigger a *FINAL SUN email* by creating or updating a {{SUN - Final}} PMT Case.
# Check the *email received* in Outlook/Gmail.
# Compare the *Client Start Up Time* value in the email with the one on the PNC record.

h3. *Actual Result:*

* The *PNC record* displays time as: {{12:15:00 AM}}
* The *FINAL SUN email* displays time as: {{00:15:00.000Z}}
* The formats do not match.

h3. *Expected Result:*

The *Client Start Up Time* displayed in the *FINAL SUN email* should match the format used in Salesforce ({{hh:mm:ss AM/PM}}).

!image-20251120-144505.png|width=670,alt="image-20251120-144505.png"!



!image-20251120-144515.png|width=670,alt="image-20251120-144515.png"!

1|i0hcrv: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM *Detailed Description:* Completed a rate change notice through Agentforce for one EE NPPA (as we're working on loading larger amount of executed contracts into environment). Template generated is missing data and not all expected results met. Detailed below.

Can someone please provide user stories for Rate Change Notices through Agentforce?

*Tester:* Maci Broussard

*Role/Persona:* Clinician Contracting Specialist

*Test Case ID:* AF-009

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Log in as Matt Hebert
2. Select Agentforce icon
3. Typed "Initiate a rate change notice at AdventHealth ER Winter Haven - EM"
4. Verified Global ID 496464
5. Agentforce provided Client Contract #, I confirmed and selected Submit
6. Agentforce provided list of Clinicians, I selected only Kaycie Samantha Stringer and clicked Submit
7. Typed "Generate Preview letters"
8. Agent provided link (link sent me to Contract Record in draft and I was able to preview the letter under Files
9. Previewed File: Note File was not generated properly, but I continued with steps. Missing Service Line and table data (image)
10. Confirmed Preview Letter by typing "Yes" and received successful confirmation
11. Never received email notification with notice attached (my scp email is on Kaycie Samantha Stringer's Person Account)
12. Contract Record with Rate Change notice attached still in Draft status and previous Executed Contract still Executed with no End Date. (not deactivated)
13. Rates are on the Contract Record with Rate Change notice but did not pull into notice letter. Additionally, I would have expected to provide the agent with new rates.. not use all rates from previous Executed contract.
14. Cannot find any Task/Activity/Case created on Person Account of Kaycie (expecting closed Task with the Subject = "Email - Clinician Rate Change Notice")

NOTE: in addition to agent not asking for rate configuration, it never asked me for the effective date of Rate Change Notice. Should it always be using the most recent Client Contract Record?

*Expected Vs Actual:* All detailed above.

*Record Link:* [https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000LOKWAAA5/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000QJ13FAAT%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000LOKWAAA5/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000QJ13FAAT%2Fview]



*Attachments:* Please find attached below.

!Kaycie Stringer Notice Letter - Agentforce.png|width=533,alt="Kaycie Stringer Notice Letter - Agentforce.png"!

1. From the Corporate Support App, click on the "Accounts" tab
2. Locate a Service Account where Medical Director exists or add a Medical Director to an Account
3. Click the button in the header for "Leadership Change"
4. Select "Adjust Current Leadership" and click "Next"

1|i0hcor: 05/Feb/26 4:06 PM *Detailed Description:*

OFF-001 _Initiate Voluntary Offboarding (Facility Only)_Job req for back fill is not created in Draft status once after termination is initiated

*Tester:* Geetha Bhavani Vanam

*Role/Persona:* Medical Staff Liaison

*Test Case ID:* OFF-001

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Navigate to Person Account of the Clinician you'd like to terminate from Global Search
2. From the Clinician Person Account, Select the down arrow at the top of the page and select "Initiate Termination Request"
3. Select 1 or more (but not all) related Facilities, and click "Next"
4. On next screen select down arrow and choose "Edit"
5. Populate the "Date Termination Received" and "Last Day"
6. Check the box for "Voluntary Termination"
7.Choose "Reason for Termination"
8. Check the box for "Backfill Recommended"
9. Save and click "Next"

*Expected vs Actual Result:*

1. User can select relevant HPFs ----Pass
2. User can determining if this is a Term from 1 or more Facilities or from SCP in general---Pass
3. Adding attachments, if applicable--pass
4. User can populate required fields for Termination Dates and Reasons and if a backfill is needed--Pass
5. A Facility Offboarding parent Case is created for each HPF--Pass
6. Three Activities are generated against the parent Case: "Update Records and Notify Teams", "QGenda Credentialling Inactivation" and "Update Malpractice Insurance" (NOTE: Additional activities may be generated if certain criteria are met)--Pass
7. Job Req for Backfill is auto-created in "Draft" status---+*Fail*+"

*Record Link:* _Not provided in UAT feedback form._

*Attachment:* Please find attached below.

 Karthika D 0 Karthika D 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 24/Nov/25 10:06 AM   2 19/Nov/25 8:00 AM SCP Sprint 12 21/Nov/25 9:33 AM Initially, the condition was set incorrectly, causing the entire Job Application list to hide when a single application was withdrawn. Coding: Logic Errors
1|i0hc5v: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM h3. *Description:*

When withdrawing one job application, the system incorrectly withdraws *all active job applications*.
Both jobs belong to *different service accounts* and are *independent*, so withdrawing one should not affect the other.

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Log in to the *HC Portal* as a user with multiple active job applications (each under a different service account).
# Navigate to the *Job Applications* page.
# Select one job application and click *Withdraw*.
# Return to the *Home Page* or *Job Application List* view.

h3. *Actual Result:*

All active job applications are withdrawn after withdrawing just one job.

h3. *Expected Result:*

Only the *selected job application* should be withdrawn; other active applications should remain unaffected.

h3. *Test data :* [https://corehc--qa.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001gP00000A8RWjQAN/view|https://corehc--qa.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Account/001gP00000A8RWjQAN/view|smart-link]



Screenshot : I have attached one video refer last part

Regression bug _Withdrawing one job removes all active applications instead of only the selected one on HC Portal

None

1|i0hbqz: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM h3. *Description:*

When initiating onboarding for *SCP* from the Clinician Account, an extra *Facility* section is appearing in the portal.
As per the requirement, *only the SCP section* should be visible under the Onboarding section.

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Log in to Salesforce as an internal user.
# Navigate to the *Clinician Account* record.
# From the *SCP* form, initiate the onboarding process.
# Log in to the *Clinician Portal* using the same user credentials.
# Navigate to the *Onboarding* section.

h3. *Actual Result:*

The portal displays an extra *Facility section* along with the SCP section (refer to screenshot below).

h3. *Expected Result:*

Only the *SCP section* should be displayed in the onboarding screen; no extra Facility section should appear.

 Kristin Koide PR1050558-4238, PR1050558-4239 AC1: Record Triggered Flow in Object Task, update the Client Change Management records

AC2: Update Sharing Setting in Field

AC3: Enable Tracking History 		0 Kristin Koide 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Auto update the Client Change Management record Status
Given I am a Clinician Experience Specialist within the Corporate Support apps
When a Client Change Management record is created with Process Type = Manual and Status = New (original story for task creation [+PR1050558-1682+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1682]+.+ Related story for additional CCM record creation [PR1050558-3574|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3574])
And I receive a task subject = “Create New Clinician Contract for [Clinician’s Name]”
And I update the Task Status
Then

* If Task Status = ‘Completed’, then the system shall update the related Client Change Management record Status = ‘Completed’
* If Task Status = ‘Canceled’, then the system shall update the related Client Change Management record Status = ‘Canceled’


AC2: Update Sharing Setting
Given I am a System Administrator
When I am in Setup
And Object = Client Change Management
Then the Sharing Setting for ‘Contract’ field is set to:

* Read Only: Allows users with at least Read access to the Master record to create, edit, or delete related Detail records.



AC3: Enable History Tracking for ‘Status’
Given I am a System Administrator
When I am in Setup
And Object = Client Change Management
Then History Tracking is enabled for Field = 'Status' 		02/Feb/26 3:51 PM   3 17/Nov/25 1:21 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy), SCP Sprint 12 18/Nov/25 10:08 AM 1|i0h83v: 10/Feb/26 12:30 PM As a Clinician Experience Specialist, when I close my task “Create New Clinician Contract for [Clinician’s Name]', then I want the system to update the status of the related Client Change Management record, so that when users report on the Client Change Management records they know if it was completed or canceled.

 Kristin Koide AC 1
In the existing Applicant_After_Insert_After_Update_Flow,
//Check if inactive user exists for that Applicant Account.
Get user record where AccountId = Applicant Id ({!$Record.bpats__ATS_Applicant__r.Account.}) and User.IsActive = false.
if record exists, update the user record by setting Active = true and Email = {!$Record.bpats__ATS_Applicant__r.Account.Email}
Add Send email action with Reset password template specified in AC

AC 4
On the existing flow, that sends out platform event, update the condition to include HPF status _Candidate in OR condition_


!image-20251113-161209.png|width=817,alt="image-20251113-161209.png"!




 0 Kristin Koide 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Story split from 3573, this story is to track *testing only* of AC2 & AC3. *Dependent on Okta Team to complete work in QA.*

– For AC 2 &3 can be tested in an environment where Okta is set up. No work in SF, just needs to be tested in QA. Need to coordinate with the Okta team - Sravan Kumar Naladala –

AC2: Remove SSO Permission
Given I am a Clinician that left SCP
When Okta deactivated the Experience Cloud User
Then Okta shall also ensure that the SSO Permission set has been removed

AC3: Add SSO Permission
Given I am a Clinician (Active) that has an executed contract and contract’s related HPF(s) Provider System Status = ‘Active’ {color:#bf2600}*or ‘Candidate’*{color} (part of story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614]+,+ update in {color:#bf2600}red{color} based on AC4)
When Okta has update the ‘Federation ID’ & ‘Email’ fields on the Experience Cloud User
Then Okta shall ensure that the SSO Permission set has been added
And from Portal Login, the user will input their email
And system directs to the SSO login (story [+PR1050558-3546+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3546])



----

NOTE: SF work AC 1 & AC 4 are part of story [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3573|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3573|smart-link]

AC1: Reactivate Experience Cloud User in Salesforce

AC4: Given I am an Integration User in the HC org (updates story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614]+,+ updates in {color:#bf2600}red{color})

Assumptions:

# Upon initial User Deactivation:
## Okta will deactivate the Okta User
## Okta will deactivate the Experience Cloud User & remove the access.
## The SCP Team has either automatically or manually cleared the Experience Cloud User’s ‘Federation ID’ field for Phase 1.
# Upon reapply/rehire:
## The Clinician has one person account linked to one user record
# Upon Clinician contract Executed: (see story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614])
## The SCP/Okta Team has updated the Create API to reactivate the Okta User via the Global Provider ID.
### If there is a Global Provider ID, then Okta will send the appropriate activation email.
### If there is NO Global Provider ID, then Okta will create the new user and appropriate email is sent
## Okta activates Okta User
## Okta updates the Experience Cloud User’s ‘Federation ID’ & ‘Email’

----

REFINEMENT NOTES

{color:#bf2600}Question:{color}

# {color:#bf2600}Jose - As part of AC3 which is related to story {color}[https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614|smart-link]{color:#bf2600}, as part of our new criteria trigger the platform event, in addition to the Clinician’s Contract Status = Executed, we are also checking if contracts {color}{color:#bf2600}*related HPF(s) ‘Provider System Status’ field to equal ‘Active’. Are we sure that we want to include this new criteria?*{color}{color:#bf2600} {color}{color:#0747a6}*11/11, we will leave this criteria on story 3614. Jose will check with business if this criteria needs to be adjusted and if so will adjust ‘Provider System Status’ formula field to make the status = “Active” at the right time.*{color}{color:#0747a6} {color}

{color:#bf2600}There is current automation the HPF ‘Status’ is set to ‘Active’, when the ‘First Clinical Shift’ is populated. However, it is towards the end of the onboarding process. It seems a bit late in the process to wait for the 'Provider System Status’ field to equal ‘Active’ to provision Okta.{color}

Screenshot showing where Contract Status = Executed and where the HPF *Status* = Active.

!image-20251107-192120.png|width=1017,height=183,alt="image-20251107-192120.png"!

Also the E2E says Okta SSO is provisioned when Contract Status = Executed (screenshot below is a close up shot of the first green box).

!image-20251107-192719.png|width=313,height=183,alt="image-20251107-192719.png"! 		02/Feb/26 10:35 AM   5 image-20251107-192120.png 17/Nov/25 9:56 AM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy), SCP Sprint 12 17/Nov/25 9:56 AM 1|i0h4ob: 02/Feb/26 10:35 AM As a System Administrator, when a Clinician leaves SCP (deactivated) and decides to reapply/rehire, I want the system to reactivate the Clinician’s previous Experience Cloud User record instead of creating a new user record, so that we keep our system clean.

As a System Administrator, I want to ensure that when a clinician’s user has the appropriate permission, the system can direct them to the appropriate sign-in method. 		14/Nov/25

Tech - SSO Flow - TESTING ONLY - Okta to Add/Remove SSO Permission

1|i0h4dv: 05/Feb/26 4:02 PM *Detailed Description:* Initiated Voluntary Offboarding for clinician below and Employee Review task was created in error. Voluntary Termination is being selected, per the video, but it's not getting saved on the record.

*Tester:* Jaclyn Gonzalez

*Persona:* Clinician Experience Specialist

*Steps to reproduce:*

1. Navigated to Daniel S. Kirschner Person Account
2. From the Clinician Person Account, Select the down arrow at the top of the page and select "Initiate Termination Request"
3. Select all Facilities, select "Full SCP Termination" and click "Next"
4. On next screen select down arrow next to the HPF and choose "Edit"
5. Populate the "Date Termination Received" and "Last Day"
6. Check the box for "Voluntary Termination"
7.Choose "Reason for Termination"
8. Save and click "Next"
9. Repeated Steps 4-8 for each HPF

*Expected vs Actual:* "Would have expected the Employee Review task under SCP Offboarding to NOT be created. Criteria for when this should be created is below. In video, user is selecting Voluntary Termination but it's not shown on the Case Details record as TRUE.

'- System Auto generates when parent case = "SCP Offboarding" is created
AND Voluntary_Termination__c = FALSE
AND Employment Type = Independent Contractor

*Record link:* [https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Case/500bf00000LEDB5AAP/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000QIxvUAAT%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Case/500bf00000LEDB5AAP/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000QIxvUAAT%2Fview|smart-link]

No permission
 Karthika D 0 Karthika D Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 14/Nov/25 5:47 AM   1 14/Nov/25 5:21 AM SCP Sprint 12 Invalid defect 1|i0h2gj: 14/Nov/25 5:47 AM *ISSUE 1*

h3. *Description*

When viewing a *Letter of Intent Contract* with *Status = Executed*, the user is incorrectly able to *edit fields in the Contract Overview section and the Letter of Intent Request Info section*.
This violates Acceptance Criteria which states that these fields should be *read-only* once LOI Status is not Draft or Submitted.

h3. *Expected Behavior*

For LOI Contract *Status = Executed*:

* Contract Overview section fields → *Read Only*
* LOI Request Info section (Left column) → *Read Only*
* LOI Request Info section (Right column) → *Read Only*
* Only Key Dates section (limited fields) should remain editable.

h3. *Actual Behavior*

* User is able to *edit* Contract Overview fields in Executed status.
* User is able to *edit* LOI Request Info (Left & Right column) fields in Executed status.

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Log in as a *Legal Team Member*.
# Navigate to *Corporate Support App → Contracts tab*.
# Open an LOI Contract where *Status = Executed*.
# Click *Edit*.
# Observe that fields in Contract Overview + LOI Request Info are still editable.

h3. *Impact*

Legal team may unintentionally modify finalized LOI data, causing compliance and documentation risks.

*ISSUE 2*

h3. *Description*

The field *Source Opportunity* is editable in *all LOI Status values*, including Executed, Draft, and Submitted.
This violates AC2 which states:

{quote}“Source Opportunity should NOT be editable in any status.”{quote}

h3. *Expected Behavior*

*Source Opportunity must be NON-editable* at all times (all statuses).

h3. *Actual Behavior*

* Source Opportunity remains *editable* across all statuses (Draft, Submitted, Executed).

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Log in as *Legal Team Member*.
# Open any Letter of Intent Contract.
# Click *Edit*.
# Observe that *Source Opportunity* field is editable regardless of status.

h3. *Impact*

Incorrect linkage between Opportunity → LOI Contract may occur, creating data integrity issues.

LOI Contract – Fields Editable in Executed Status (Not Expected)

None

19/Nov/25 1:38 AM Unified Platform Phase 1 Release Done Client 360 Done   Medium   PR1050558-2472 nawal.bedmutha Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-4143
No permission
 Pratik Kedar h3. *Create a Platform Event:*

* Name: Clone Record
* API Name: Clone_Record__e
* Field
** Id of Record To Clone
** sObject Name
** Attribute 1
** Attribute 2
** Attribute 3



h3. Create Platform Event:

***Remove the Existing Logic to Create the Job Record for* [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2472|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2472|smart-link] ****

Case RT: Clinician Offboarding

When Case.Backfill_Recommended__c is updated and set as True and Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.Job_Applicant__r.bpats__Job__c is not NULL

Create the Platform Event: Clone Record

* Row Id of Record To Clone = Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.Job_Applicant__r.bpats__Job__c
* sObject Name = bpats__Job__c
* Attribute 1 = If Case.Voluntary_Termination__c = TRUE, Attrition - Resignation, Else Attrition - Termination



h3. Create Job Record:

Create a Platform Triggered Flow

Setup Run This Flow as: Default Workflow User

Assign Permission to Default Workflow User: Mulesoft Asymbl Integration

When sObject Name = bpats__Job__c Then

*CLONE* *ALL Field* the bpats__Job__c record Id = PlatformEvent.Id of Record To Clone

Set fields

* bpats__Job__c.bpats__Status__c = Draft
* bpats__Job__c.bpats__Post_Externally__c = FALSE
* bpats__Job__c.bpats__Posted_Date__c = null
* bpats__Job__c.Vacancy_Reason__c = PlatformEvent.Attribute 1



SendEmail to VPO as per AC from Platform Event Flow

1|i0gz8r: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM Related to story [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2906|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2906|smart-link]

*Expected Result:* when the SFO selects ‘Specify Rates’ on the first screen Type of Rate = Shift and user to specify Shift Type to proceed. (AC in Red failed)

Given I am a SFO in the Corporate Support Apps
When I select *Shift Coverage Change ONLY*
Once the Practice Operations Contract has been cloned
Then I am only able to create new Shift records with the *same shift type*, and *add rates (no rate changes*) for newly created shifts that match the same shift type
And I am able to add/remove Rate Pay Codes to my newly added Shift records
And on Summary tab, when I select Specify Rates, then:

* {color:#bf2600}'{color}{color:#bf2600}*Type of Rate*{color}{color:#bf2600}' field I can only select “Shift”{color}
** I should NOT have the ability to add or edit any other type of rate (Variable, Leadership Incentive, Stipend, Target)
* On Rate Details screen, for ‘*Type*' I should ONLY see the Shift Full Name(s) of the newly add Shifts (same Shift Type)
* On Rate Details screen, for ‘*Type*' I should ONLY able to add new Shift for existing Facility Title already available with the same Shift Type
* On Rate Details screen, for ‘*Rate*’ the ONLY value that can be inputted is same rate.
** Error message: The ‘Rate' must match the previous rate entered for the selected Facility Title(s) & Shift Type(s). Select the Facility Title in such a way the rates will match.

Screen shot from original QA testing where it worked


!image-20251112-170854.png|width=1017,alt="image-20251112-170854.png"!

*Actual Result*: when the SFO selects ‘Specify Rates’ on the first screen ‘Type of Rate’ = Shift and ‘Shift Type’ field are missing.

Test user = Richard Caramel
Test Contract = *00008824*

!image-20251112-170934.png|width=1017,alt="image-20251112-170934.png"!

13/Nov/25 11:10 AM Unified Platform Phase 1 Release Done Done   Medium   PR1050558-887 nawal.bedmutha Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-4130
No permission
 Karthika D 0 Karthika D Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 13/Nov/25 11:10 AM   1 12/Nov/25 9:31 AM SCP Sprint 12 1|i0gyrf: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM h3. Description

The automated "Preliminary Sun Notification Report" and "FINAL Sun Notification Report" and “Sun Revision email “ emails are being sent with multiple blank fields. This prevents stakeholders from receiving complete information. The issues appear related, as many of the same fields (e.g., Malpractice Insurance, Corporate Entity, Client Start Up Date) are missing from _all 3_ emails.

h3. Steps to Reproduce (STR)



# Log in to *Salesforce* as an *Implementation Specialist* user.
# Navigate to *App Launcher → Corporate Support App → PMT Cases*.
# Click *New* to create a *PMT Case* record.
# Set the *PMT Type* = “SUN - Preliminary”
# Repeat Steps 2–7 with *PMT Type = “SUN - Preliminary”* and *“SUN - Revision”*.
# Open the received *Final Sun Notification Email*.

h3. *4. Expected vs. Actual Results*

h4. *Issue 1 – Preliminary Startup Notification Report*

* *Expected Result:*
The email should be fully populated with all required data fields from the PMT Phase, Service Account, and related Contract.
* *Actual Result:*
The following fields are *blank*:
** Medical Malpractice Insurance Limits per Occurrence
** Medical Malpractice Insurance Aggregate
** Corporate Entity
** *Client Owner*
** *Annual ED Billable Volume*
** *Annual HM Patient Encounters*
** *Tele Services Active* is showing No it should be yes (Condition : Display “Yes” if “TeleMedicine”is Greater than '0')

h4. *Issue 2 – Final Startup Notification Report*

* *Expected Result:*
The email should display complete and accurate field values, including those from the *related PNC* and *Client Contract* records.
* *Actual Result:*
The following fields are *blank*:
** Annual ED Billable Volume
** Annual HM Patient Encounters
** Service line
** Medical Malpractice Insurance Limits per Occurrence
** Medical Malpractice Insurance Aggregate
** Corporate Entity
** Client Start Up Date
** Client Start Up Time
** Client Start Up Time Zone
** *Tele Services Active* is showing No it should be yes (Condition : Display “Yes” if “TeleMedicine”is Greater than '0')

h4. *Issue 3 – Startup Revision Notification Email*

* *Expected Result:*
Revision emails should pull the same data fields as the *SUN - Preliminary* Notification.
* *Actual Result:*
Same data gaps observed — several key fields missing from the email body.

Sun Notification Emails (Preliminary, Final & Revision) Missing Key Data Fields

None

1|i0gxnn: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM Issue - There can be multiple service lines in Action plan templates Task, delete the tasks that do not have any of the service lines mentioned in Service account level.
But as of current behaviour : The system does text to text compare and deletes the task records that does not have exact match for the service lines in service account records.

Steps to Reproduce:-

1.Create a Service Account and ensure Service Line exists.

* Account team role : Implementation Project director.


2.Create Opportunity RT: Growth Opp. -Link to Service Account #1

* Related:create Services record. link to Service Account.

# Create PMT project record.

* Associate PMT project Lookup on Opportunity to PMT Project & PMT project.
* Create Phases.


--Under phases, create tasks

# Create Action plan template record. with the same name of Task name (#3)

* Create task under action plan template and ensure Service Line exists
* Publish the action plan template.

# PMT project > Phases -> Update Service Account field.
# All 5 tasks are created.
# Now change the status of PMT Phase tasks to In Progress.
# Now navigate back to Service account record and verify ACT Tasks are now displayed on Service Account record.

The system does text to text compare and deletes the task records that does not have exact match for the service lines

 Samudhraa Samudhraa PR1050558-4248, PR1050558-4249 0 Samudhraa Samudhraa 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Ensure when PCAs are deleted ( because shifts on PO Contract are deleted or PO PCA is deleted or any other scenario), the rates are recalculated and reflect only the existing PCA records that are applicable.

Given I am a Payroll team member in the Corporate Support apps
When EE NP/PA rates are specified for a clinician (using per hour unit of measure)
Then the following calculations are done automatically:

* EE NP/PA Full Time Weekly Blended
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility * 40
* EE NP/PA Hourly Blended
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility HPFs
* EE NP/PA Full Time Overage Rate
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility HPFs + $2
* EE NP/PA Full Time Monthly Blended
** EE NP/PA Hourly Blended * NP/PA Tier

*see the attached document*
*The NPPA rates only apply to Shift records, where Shift are of Unit of measure = per hour*

additional notes:
_FT EE NP/PA clinicians have one monthly salary blended in contract_
_PT EE NP/PA clinicians have one hourly blended rate in contract_
_for FT and PT - QGenda stores blended hourly rate_
_for FT - Workday stores annual salary_
_for PT - Workday stores hourly blended rate_
if clinician has Night Shift Bonus, sent to QGenda and applies only for some shifts

[^Calculations.xlsx]

 Dipendra Singh Rathore 0 Dipendra Singh Rathore Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 07/Nov/25 2:52 PM   7 image-20251107-192148.png 06/Nov/25 12:21 PM SCP Sprint 12 06/Nov/25 1:59 PM The defect preventing the viewing of CTI records and critical contract details (e.g., individual vs. entity contracting) was caused by a *missing or incomplete configuration of user permissions*.

* *Configuration Error:* The intended users lacked the required access rights to view the CTI records and associated contract fields. The necessary permissions were not bundled or assigned to the users' profiles/permission sets during previous deployments.
Requirements: Incomplete Requirements
1|i0gs3r: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM *Detailed Description:* Provider Contracting members, specifically the specialists, need to be able to view CTI records from the Person record, and we need to be able to see whether the clinician is contracting individually or through a business entity, and the name of said entity from the Contract and Agreement records. We have no way of verifying or identifying it otherwise.

for the Contract/Agreement records, if they are contracting individually, then the record should have a field that says Individual and a field that shows what the signature block entry will look like (John Doe, MD)

If they are contracting through an entity, then the record should have a field that says Entity, and a field that shows what the signature block entry will show, (ABC Corp, LLC), clinician's name and title of Authorized Signatory should pull into the Signature block, but does not need to be shown on the Contract/Agreement records

*Persona:* Clinician Contracting Specialist

*Tester:* Matthew Hebert

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Navigate to the Clinician - Approved and Term Sheet Exectd list view. Select the Contract record for Brad Allen Blaker. You will not be able to tell that he has an entity.

*Expected vs Actual:*

See above in detailed description section.

*Record link:* [+https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000KsdSQAAZ/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000QJ27mAAD%2Fview+|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000KsdSQAAZ/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000QJ27mAAD%2Fview]

 Dipendra Singh Rathore 0 Dipendra Singh Rathore Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 07/Nov/25 1:15 PM   7 Approval_Work_Item_Not_Created.png 06/Nov/25 10:54 AM SCP Sprint 12 06/Nov/25 11:35 AM The reported defect concerning the missing approval work item after contract submission was due to a *misunderstanding of the approved business process requirements* for specific contract types.

* The original test scripts and user expectation *assumed* that *leased and locum contracts* required a formal approval work item generated and routed to the Tactical Team Queue.
* *Intended Logic Confirmed:* The system behavior was confirmed to be correct, i.e., leased and locum contracts are designed to *bypass the approval process* and transition directly to the *"Executed" status* because they do not involve special terms requiring review.
* *Resolution:* The issue was closed after review confirmed the system was functioning as intended. The test scripts were updated to align with the correct process, excluding leased/locum contracts from the "Data Setup Required" and approval testing conditions.
Requirements: Misunderstood Requirements
1|i0grrr: 05/Feb/26 4:06 PM Approval Work Item Not Generated After Contract Submission for leased and locum as Clinician

*Persona:* Recruiter

*Tester*: Nikhil Kokate

*Test Case ID:* CONT-007,CONT-008,CONT-009,CONT-010

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. From the Person Account you created in CONT-001, click on the Agreements tab
2. Drill into the Contract Number for a Contract in Status = Draft
3. Add notes into ""Contract Special Terms"" and Save
4. Click on the Summary tab
5. Click ""Submit Contract Request""
_Verify that Contract was Submitted_
6. Click on the Approval tab
7. Validate that an Approval Work Item has been created

*Observation*: The contract status is updated to "Submitted," but no Approval Work Item is created under the "Approval" tab. The item is not assigned to the Tactical Team Queue.

*Expected Results*

# User can add Contract Special Terms to a Draft Contract

# User can Submit Contract for Approval

# Contract gets Submitted and Assigned to the Tactical Team Queue

*Expected Result:*
An Approval Work Item should be created and visible under the "Approval" tab, assigned to the Tactical Team Queue.

*Actual Result:*The contract status is updated to "Submitted," but no Approval Work Item is created under the "Approval" tab. The item is not assigned to the Tactical Team Queue.

UAT Bug: C&CUAT96 Approval Work Item Not Generated After Contract Submission for leased and locum as Clinician

1|i0grr3: 05/Feb/26 4:06 PM *Detailed Description:* ONB-004 Process Manual onboarding Facility only
As per Expected results

# For the Hospital Orientation child case, the related Task auto-closes once User fills in the "First Clinical Shift" on the HPF

but the task is not auto closed.

*Persona:* Medical Staff Liaison

*Tester:* Geetha Bhavani Vanam


*Steps to Reproduce:*

Login as CET

# From Person Account in ONB-003, click on Cases
# In the Open Onboarding related list, drill into the "Facility Onboarding - <Facility Name> Case
# Click on the Related Tab and view the Child Cases, which can include:
-MSS Onboarding
-Hospital Application Preparation
-Hospital Privileging
-Payer Enrollment
-Hospital Orientation
# Drill into the "Hospital Orientation" child case
# Drill into the related Task for "Schedule Hospital Orientation"
# Navigate to the related HPF (Person Account -> Facilities -> HPF) and Fill in "First Clinical Shift" and Save"

*Expected Result* : For the Hospital Orientation child case, the related Task auto-closes once User fills in the "First Clinical Shift" on the HPF

*Actual Result* : Schedule Hospital Orientation task is not auto closed once First clinical shift is updated"

1|i0grnz: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM h3. *Description:*

When viewing a *Clinician Contract* record in the *Summary tab*, the *Delete icon (trash can)* for the HPF record card appears *outside the border* of the HPF tile.
This creates a *UI misalignment* and gives the impression that the button is detached from the component.

This issue is observed consistently for HPFs displayed in the *Summary tab* (e.g., VN Hospital HPF).

h3. *Expected Result:*

The *Delete icon* should be *aligned within the HPF card border*, maintaining consistent spacing and visual alignment with other card elements.

h3. *Actual Result:*

The *Delete icon* overlaps the card border and is displayed *outside the visual container*, breaking layout consistency.

!image-20251106-151446.png|width=670,alt="image-20251106-151446.png"!

Regression bug: Delete icon for HPF appears misaligned and displayed outside the card border in the Clinician Contract Summary tab

1|i0gr3r: 29/Jan/26 9:03 PM Tester:

Persona:

Test Case ID: CONT-0035a

Description: I should have been able to select the rates and proceed with requesting the contract but the error prevented me from being able to proceed with the request.

1. Navigate to Richard Miller's Person Account (Job 00257 -> ATS Kanban tab -> Offer section -> Click on ATS Applicant's name -> Drill on Applicant OR search the name using the global search)
2. Click on the down arrow in the Quick Action area of the page and choose "Contract Request"
3. Choose Employee Type of "Employee"
4. Choose Time Type of Full Time
5. Click "Next"
6. Enter a Contract Start Date
7. Click on "Start New Request" and click "Next"
8. Select Otsego HM HPF plus any others you want to include and click "Next"
9. Navigate to the Summary tab
10. Select Configure Rates
11. Select Shift for Type of Rate and click Next
12. Modify selection by deselecting what does not apply
13. Select Finish

I have some sites that have a $0 rate that pulls into the contract request, along with an hourly rate. This is so that I can select whether the physician will be working onsite shifts or the site just needs to be added to the contract for the purpose of being privileged/contracted there for tele backup, but will never work an actual onsite shift so doesn't need an hourly rate. The error was occurring for EE physicians only and didn't seem to be an issue for IC physicians but they should have the $0 rate pull in as well. When I add shift rates, it causes an error (See screenshot) but even if I uncheck the $0 rates, I cannot proceed. I click Finish and nothing happens.

UAT Bug: C&CUAT89 $0 Rates causing error

Data Model for Client Contract

Fix for issue from [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-887|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-887|smart-link]

Query 4: SOQL Select Occurrence_Limit__c, Annual_Aggregate__c FROM Contract_Provider_Network_Relation__c WHERE Contract__r.Contract_Type__c = “Insurance” AND Provider_Network_Contract__r.Contract.SourceOpportunityId = inov8__PMT_Phase__r.inov8__Project__r.Opportunity__c AND Provider_Network_Contract__r.*Service_Account__c* = inov8__PMT_Phase__c.Service_Account__c AND Contract__r.ParentContractId = Query1.ContractId {color:#ff991f}AND {color}Provider_Network_Contract__r{color:#ff991f}.Contract.Agreement_Category__c = “Facility Agreement”{color} Order By Provider_Network_Contract__r.Contract.StartDate DESC LIMIT 1



!image-20251119-143850.png|width=1017,alt="image-20251119-143850.png"! 		0 Kristin Koide 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Add new ‘Agreement Category' = *'Facility Agreement'*
Given I am a Legal team member within the Corporate Support apps
When I am working with a Contract (RT: Client Contract)
And Contract Type = 'Master’
Then for field 'Agreement Category' (see list below - values to add and values to inactivate)

List of values: 

# Affiliation Agreement  (*NEW*)
# -Assistant Medical Director Agreement- (inactive value)
#   BAA - Facility
#   -BAA - Provider- (inactive value)
#   BAA - Vendor
#   -Business Associate Agreement- (inactive value)
# Confidentiality Agreement (*NEW*) 
# Consulting
# Corporate Lease Agreement (*NEW*)
#   -EE NP Agreement- (inactive value)
#   -EE NP Lead MLP Agreement- (inactive value)
#   -EE PA Agreement-(inactive value)
#   -EE PA Load MLP Agreement- (inactive value)
#   -EE Physician- (inactive value)
# *Facility Agreement (NEW)*
# GME Agreement (*NEW*)
# Hospice Agreement (*NEW*)
#   -Hospital Medicine Program Administrator- (inactive value)
#   -Hospital Medicine Program Director- (inactive value)
#   -Lead Mid Level Agreement- (inactive value)
# Leased Clinician Agreement (*NEW*)
#   -Medical Director Agreement- (inactive value)
#   Other
#   -Provider Agreement- (inactive value)
#   -Safety- (inactive value)
#   -Wellness- (inactive value)
#   -Parent Provider Agreement- (inactive value)
# Subcontractor Clinician Agreement *(NEW)*

(NOTE: Inactive values will NOT display in the Client Contract’s Agreement Category picklist. We are NOT deleting any values.)

AC2: Add "Agreement Category" to the highlights panel
Given I am a Legal team member within the Corporate Support apps
When I am working with a Contract (RT: Client Contract)
And Contract Type = Master
Then field = "Agreement Category" is visible within the highlights panel (add after Contract Record Type)

* Contract Number
* Account Name
* Status
* Contract Start Date
* Contract Record Type
* Agreement Category (*NEW*)

AC3: Update FINAL SUN Email Alert (original story [+PR1050558-887+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-887])
Given I am a Implementation Specialist within the Corporate Support Apps
When a ‘SUN - Final’ PMT Case is created
Then for values to display that reference the Client Contract record, the system shall use the Client Contract meeting the following criteria:

* Contract Record Type = Client Contract
* Contract Type = Master (note is the field holds the different child sections as well ex. Term & Renewals, Compensation, Protection, etc. Master represents the top level)
* Agreement Category = *Facility Agreement*

Assumption (confirmed by Nicole): the Service Account will have at least one Client Contract with Agreement Category = “Facility Agreement”.

AC4: Update the "All Client Contracts" list view: (original story [+PR1050558-2188+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2188])

When I am on the Contract tab
Then I can view the "All Client Contracts" list view

* Update Filter: add filter "Contract Type" = "Master" 
* Add column = *Agreement Category*
** Contract Number
** Account Name
** Status
** Contract Start Date
** Contract End Date
** Contract Owner Alias
** Contract Record Type
** *Agreement Category*

Then I can view the "Open Client Contract Requests" list view

* Add column = *Agreement Category*
** Contract Number
** Account Name
** Status
** Contract Start Date
** Contract End Date
** Contract Record Type
** *Agreement Category*

 Karthika D 0 Karthika D Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 06/Nov/25 1:36 AM   1 image-20251105-115605.png 05/Nov/25 6:57 AM SCP Sprint 12 Invalid defect 1|i0gohb: 06/Nov/25 1:36 AM h3. *Description:*

Certain conditional sections and fields (as defined in the *Data Dictionary – SCP Health*) are not visible on *Client Contract* records in Salesforce, despite the Contract Record Type and Contract Type meeting the required display conditions.

This issue prevents users from viewing or populating key fields such as:

* *Termination Notice Issue Date*
* *Financial Model*
* *Volume Terms*
* *Non-Compete*

Steps

* Log in as *System Administrator*
* Navigate to *Corporate Support Apps → Contracts tab*.
* Create a new *Client Contract* record OR open an existing one (Record Type = Client Contract).
* Update *Contract Type* field to *“Compensation”*/“Protection.”/“Insurance.”/“Termination.”/“Special Terms.”
* Verify the corresponding session visibility

h3. *Expected Result:*

When *Contract Type* changes, the respective conditional *section layout* (Compensation, Protection, Insurance, Special Terms, or Termination) should appear dynamically, displaying the mapped fields per Data Dictionary definition.



h3. *Actual Result:*

The *conditional sections are not rendering* on the Client Contract record page. Fields such as _Termination Notice Issue Date, Financial Model, Volume Terms, Non-Compete,_ etc., remain hidden even though:

* Record Type = Client Contract
* Contract Type matches display condition

!image-20251105-115639.png|width=670,alt="image-20251105-115639.png"!

 marla.pilpel 0 marla.pilpel 1 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 05/Nov/25 4:53 PM   4 HPF Inactive - Offer in Kanban.png 04/Nov/25 11:42 AM SCP Sprint 12 04/Nov/25 1:45 PM manual data correction



Detailed RCA:

*System Logic Confirmed:* The HPF was initially created correctly in *Prospect status*. However, the subsequent inactivation was caused by an *Automated Daily Job* that runs logic defined in *US 1580*. This job correctly identified that the newly created HPF met the inactivation criteria due to *blank critical fields* (e.g., {{Last Date Worked}} and {{Company Signed Date}}).

It appears the automated process updated the HPF to an Inactive status based on its current data state, which may have led to the understandable impression that there was an issue with the initial creation or Kanban step.
Requirements: Misunderstood Requirements

No permission
 marla.pilpel 0 marla.pilpel Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 04/Nov/25 4:57 PM   3 Screenshot 2025-11-04 at 12.57.57 PM.png 04/Nov/25 10:34 AM SCP Sprint 12 04/Nov/25 1:04 PM The reported issue concerning *Global IDs containing commas* in Service and Person Accounts was not a system defect but stemmed from a *misunderstanding of the current data design and the future migration plan.*

* *System Logic Confirmed:* The presence of commas in the Global IDs was confirmed to be *intentional and by design* for the current stage of the project.
* *Misconception:* The issue was raised as a bug because the current data format did not align with the perceived final, clean structure.
* *Resolution:* The team clarified that the final resolution will be handled through a planned *data type change to Auto-number* before migration to production. Since the current behavior is intentional and the issue will be managed via a future migration step, the bug was marked as *"Won't Do"* and closed.
Requirements: Misunderstood Requirements

 Dipendra Singh Rathore 0 Dipendra Singh Rathore Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 06/Nov/25 9:43 AM   3 Nicole Rice AF-007.png 31/Oct/25 11:57 AM SCP Sprint 12 31/Oct/25 3:48 PM The system is successfully identifying and retrieving the *Facility Global ID* (sourced from the active HPFs, such as those associated with Global Provider ID 46923). However, the subsequent *lookup process* that uses this ID to fetch the corresponding *EMR system details* is failing. This prevents the EMR information from being displayed, resulting in the "unavailable" message.
Coding: Logic Errors
1|i0gilr: 29/Jan/26 9:04 PM *Detailed Description:* Following script to determine what systems are being used at where the verified clinician works. Bot prompted me to select which site as there are multiple PFAs, but then once selected "EMR system information is not available for the selected Global Facility ID. Let me know if you need further assistance!"

*Persona:* Clinician Experience Specialist

*UAT Tester/Reporter:* Nicole Rice

*Test Case ID:* AF-007

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Me: what systems are being used at the facility where clinician works?
Bot: The clinician works at multiple facilities. Please select the facility to view the systems being used.

Account:
Driscoll Children's Hospital Rio Grande Valley-PEDS
494426.0

Driscoll Children's Hospital-PEDS
5539.0

I selected 494426.0 and submitted then received:

EMR system information is not available for the selected Global Facility ID. Let me know if you need further assistance!

*Expected vs Actual:* EMR system used not provided which impedes me from helping the clinician know if they need additional training, etc for the EMR in preparation for shifts onsite.

UAT Bug: C&CUAT72 NO EMR System Detected

None

-When I am on the- *-Contract (RT: Client Contract)-*

{color:#0747a6}Per the solution/design: on the client contract record, there will be a ‘Configurations’ tab. On the ‘Configurations’ tab the user can view, add, edit and delete configurations. This story has been modified to support {color}{color:#0747a6}*edit existing configurations (AC9)*{color}{color:#0747a6}. Note: the ‘Add Configuration’ button is covered under the create new client contract story {color}[{color:#0747a6}+PR1050558-3023+{color}|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3023]{color:#0747a6}. View and delete configurations are covered under story {color}[{color:#0747a6}+PR1050558-3519+{color}|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3519]{color:#0747a6}.(see mock below){color}

{color:#0747a6}AC1: (requirements split from story {color}[+PR1050558-3022+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3022]+.+ {color:#0747a6}+*Covered under story*+ {color}[{color:#0747a6}+*PR1050558-3519*+{color}|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3519]{color:#0747a6}*.*{color})

{color:#0747a6}-When I select a Client Contract-{color}
{color:#0747a6}-Then on- {color}{color:#0747a6}*-tab = Configurations-*{color}{color:#0747a6}-, I shall have the ability to view the following sections (expanded by default)-{color}

{color:#0747a6}AC9: Edit an existing records for Draft Contracts (requirements split from story {color}[+PR1050558-3023+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3023])
{color:#0747a6}Given I am a Legal Team Member within the Corporate Support Apps{color}
{color:#0747a6}When I am on the {color}{color:#0747a6}*Contract (RT: Client Contract)* {color}
{color:#0747a6}And{color} {color:#0747a6}*Status = “Draft”*{color}
{color:#0747a6}And I am on the ‘Configurations’ tab{color}
{color:#0747a6}Then I shall have the ability to “{color}{color:#0747a6}*Edit*{color}{color:#0747a6}” a specific record{color} {color:#0747a6}(if Client Contract status is ≠ Draft, then user can NOT “Edit” ){color}
{color:#0747a6}And if I select “Edit” then user can edit fields{color}
{color:#0747a6}And{color} {color:#0747a6}Navigation options are:{color}

* {color:#0747a6}Cancel {color}{color:#0747a6}_(Design: To cancel, user click on “X” in upper right of the modal)_{color}
** {color:#0747a6}No updates saved{color}
* {color:#0747a6}Next{color}

{color:#0747a6}And user has the ability to link Service Account(s){color}{color:#0747a6} {color}{color:#0747a6}[linked via Provider Network Contract (PNC) record(s)]{color}

* {color:#0747a6}Service Account(s) that can be linked, must be linked to this Contract {color}
* {color:#0747a6}Service Account(s) can NOT be linked to more than one related record of the same type. (*see example below){color}
* {color:#0747a6}Service Account(s) can be removed, but a related record MUST have at least one linked Service Account {color}

{color:#0747a6}And Navigation options are:{color}

* {color:#0747a6}Cancel {color}{color:#0747a6}_(Design: To cancel, user click on “X” in upper right of the modal)_{color}
** {color:#0747a6}No updates saved{color}
** {color:#0747a6}Back/Previous{color}
*** {color:#0747a6}User is returned to initial edit fields step with inputs retained.{color}
** {color:#0747a6}Save/Finish{color}
*** {color:#0747a6}System shall save the updates{color}

{color:#0747a6}AC10: Delete Configuration ({color}{color:#0747a6}+*Covered under story*+ {color}[{color:#0747a6}+*PR1050558-3519*+{color}|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3519]{color:#0747a6}*.*{color}{color:#0747a6}){color}
{color:#0747a6}-Given I am a Legal Team Member within the Corporate Support Apps-{color}
{color:#0747a6}-When I select a- {color}{color:#0747a6}*-Client Contract-* {color}{color:#0747a6}-record- {color}
{color:#0747a6}-And- {color}{color:#0747a6}*-Status = “Draft”-*{color}
{color:#0747a6}-And I am on the “Configurations” tab-{color}
{color:#0747a6}-Then I have the ability to “Delete Configuration” on a specific record-{color}
{color:#0747a6}-And if I select “Delete Configuration”, the system shall confirm that I want to delete.-{color}

* {color:#0747a6}-Message: Are you sure you want to delete this record?-{color}

{color:#0747a6}-And navigation options are:-{color}

* {color:#0747a6}-Cancel-{color}
** {color:#0747a6}-No records are deleted-{color}
* {color:#0747a6}-Delete-{color}
** {color:#0747a6}-Configuration record is deleted-{color}

1|i0gdef: 29/Jan/26 9:04 PM Tester: Maci Broussard

06/Nov/25 9:28 AM Unified Platform Phase 1 Release Done Provider Contracting Done   High Enhancement, SCPAgentforce, SCPHealthCloud, SCPUATEnhancement, SCPUATTicket, UATSmokeTesting   PR1050558-2811, PR1050558-3201, PR1050558-4003 Maci Broussard Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-3783

 marla.pilpel 0 marla.pilpel 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 06/Nov/25 9:28 AM   5 Term Sheet Generated.png 28/Oct/25 1:14 PM SCP Sprint 11, SCP Sprint 12 28/Oct/25 2:04 PM The defect resulting in missing data (e.g., Job Title, HPF, Compensation) on generated term sheets is due to an *incomplete scope in the initial requirements and subsequent implementation* concerning data retrieval rules.

* *Scope Limitation:* The current logic for term sheet generation was implemented only to pull *active HPF data*.
* *Missing Requirement:* The initial design failed to account for or mandate the retrieval of *inactive HPF data*, which is necessary for comprehensive term sheet generation. This gap in the requirement led to the missing fields when the source HPF records were not in an active status.

The long-term fix is dependent on completing the already-planned story *PR1050558-3201*, which will implement the logic to retrieve all HPF records regardless of status. A temporary *workaround* involves manually setting the HPF to an active status to unblock generation, pending approval for the permanent fix.
Requirements: Incomplete Requirements

 Jasmine Moradi 0 Jasmine Moradi 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Given I am a Payroll team member in the Corporate Support apps
When EE NP/PA rates are specified for a clinician (using per hour unit of measure)
Then the following calculations are done automatically:

* EE NP/PA Full Time Weekly Blended
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility * 40
* EE NP/PA Hourly Blended
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility HPFs
** {color:#0747a6}_12/30: This rate should round to 2 decimal places prior to calculating the weekly & monthly amounts_{color}
* EE NP/PA Full Time Overage Rate
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility HPFs + $2
* EE NP/PA Full Time Monthly Blended
** EE NP/PA Hourly Blended * NP/PA Tier

*see the attached document*
*The NPPA rates only apply to Shift records, where Shift are of Unit of measure = per hour*

additional notes:
_FT EE NP/PA clinicians have one monthly salary blended in contract_
_PT EE NP/PA clinicians have one hourly blended rate in contract_
_for FT and PT - QGenda stores blended hourly rate_
_for FT - Workday stores annual salary_
_for PT - Workday stores hourly blended rate_
if clinician has Night Shift Bonus, sent to QGenda and applies only for some shifts

[^Calculations.xlsx]

For Contract.RT: Practice Operation

When the User Delete the Complete Section representing the PreventiveCareAgreement grouping

*Delete:*

For each PreventiveCareAgreement record

* Cloned_From__c = Null
** Delete the Records (and the corresponding Rate_Pay_Code__c records)

!image-20251028-181613.png|width=927,alt="image-20251028-181613.png"! 		0 Kristin Koide 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Split from story [+PR1050558-2988+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2988], only items in {color:#0747a6}blue{color} are part of this story

-AC1: “Cloned From”- *-is NOT-* -populated,- *-hard delete-* -records- [Covered under split story [+PR1050558-2988+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2988]]

AC2: “Cloned From” *is* populated, *soft delete* records [Part of AC Covered under split story [+PR1050558-2988+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2988]{color:#0747a6}]{color}
{color:#0747a6}Given I am system administrator within the Corporate Support apps{color}
{color:#0747a6}When a SFO is working on a {color}{color:#0747a6}*cloned* {color}{color:#0747a6}contract (RT: {color}{color:#0747a6}*Practice Operations*{color}{color:#0747a6}){color}
{color:#0747a6}And contract status = Draft{color}
Then the user shall have the option to select the Delete option on a *Shift* record, if Deleted & “Cloned From” is populated:

* System shall update the “Is Deleted” field = True/Checked
* System shall NOT allow hard deletes
* System shall hide/prevent users from accessing the delete option if the contract status is ≠ Draft
* System shall update any related Preventive Care Agreement (*PCA*) records and {color:#0747a6}*Rate Pay Code*{color}{color:#0747a6} record(s){color} field “Is Deleted” = True/Checked

And if a Shift “Is Deleted” field = True/Checked, the user has the ability to *“Re-add”*

* -User shall update the “Is Deleted” field = False/Unchecked-
* -User shall update any related Preventive Care Agreement (-*-PCA-*-) records field via ‘Re-add’ on Summary tab (then system will update “Is Deleted” = False/Unchecked)-
* {color:#0747a6}User has the ability to {color}{color:#0747a6}*“Re-add”*{color}{color:#0747a6} the Shift (then system will update “Is Deleted” = False/Unchecked){color}
** {color:#0747a6}If Shift is updated to “Is Deleted” = True/Checked, then “Re-add” will be {color}{color:#0747a6}*hidden on related Rate Pay Codes(s) and Preventive Care Agreements (PCA) records.*{color}
** {color:#0747a6}If Shift is updated to “Is Deleted” = False/Unchecked, then the User has the {color}{color:#0747a6}*ability to “Re-add”*{color} {color:#0747a6}*any related Rate Pay Codes(s) and Preventive Care Agreements (PCA) records*{color}{color:#0747a6}. {color}

-Then the user shall have the option to select the Delete option on a- *-Rate Pay Code-* -record, if Deleted & “Cloned From” is populated:-

* -System shall update the “Is Deleted” field = True/Checked-
* -System shall NOT allow hard deletes-
* -System shall hide/prevent users from accessing the delete option if the contract status is ≠ Draft-
* -System shall update any related Rate Pay Code (-*-RPC-*-) records field “Is Deleted” = True/Checked-

-And if a Rate Pay Code (-*-RPC-*-) “Is Deleted” field = True/Checked, the user has the ability to- *-“Re-add”,-* -if Re-added:-

* -System shall update the “Is Deleted” field = False/Unchecked-

{color:#0747a6}Then on the Summary tab, the user shall have the option to select the Delete option on a {color}*-Rate and-* {color:#0747a6}*Type of Rate* {color}{color:#0747a6}record(s), if Deleted & “Cloned From” is populated: {color}

* -System shall NOT allow hard deletes-
* -System shall hide/prevent users from accessing the delete option if the contract status is ≠ Draft-
* -If the user deletes a- *-Rate-*-, then System shall update the related Preventive Care Agreement (-*-PCA-*-) record field “Is Deleted” = True/Checked [Existing functionality to delete and “Re-add”]-
** -And displays record under “Removed”-
* {color:#0747a6}If the user deletes a {color}{color:#0747a6}*Type of Rate*{color}{color:#0747a6}, then the system shall update all the related Preventive Care Agreement ({color}{color:#0747a6}*PCA*{color}{color:#0747a6}) record(s) & {color}{color:#0747a6}*Rate Pay Code* {color}{color:#0747a6}records{color} {color:#0747a6}field “Is Deleted” = True/Checked, within the selected Contract Payment Agreement ({color}{color:#0747a6}*CPA*{color}{color:#0747a6}) record {color}
** {color:#0747a6}And display Rate record(s) under “Removed” {color}

{color:#0747a6}And if a Rate (PCA) is “Is Deleted” field = True/Checked, the user has the ability to {color}{color:#0747a6}*“Re-add”* {color}{color:#0747a6}(then system will update “Is Deleted” = False/Unchecked){color}

* {color:#0747a6}If PCA is updated to “Is Deleted” = True/Checked, then “Re-add” will be {color}{color:#0747a6}*hidden on related Rate Pay Codes record(s).*{color}
* {color:#0747a6}If PCA is updated to “Is Deleted” = False/Unchecked, then the User has the {color}{color:#0747a6}*ability to “Re-add”*{color} {color:#0747a6}*any related Rate Pay Codes record(s).* {color}


*QA Testing Note: ‘Delete icon’ and ‘Re-add’ should ONLY be available when the Contract status = Draft*

1|i0g90f: 29/Jan/26 9:04 PM Issue 1 - The email subject is coming up with <P> tags.
Expected Result - The email subject shouldn't be displayed with <P> tags.
Actual Result - The email subject is coming up with <P> tags.

Issue 2 - Between the starting of the email body and main content of Pro Forma model record summary double spacing is there, it should only be one.
Expected Result - Ideally only one line spacing should be there.
Actual Result - As of now double line spacing is present.

Issue 3 - The sub points of Pro Forma Model record should be displayed as Sub points /radio buttons. But as of now all the details are coming together.
Expected Result - The radio buttons/ sub points should be displayed.
Actual Result - As of now since no sub points available so as of now in email body its looking its a one piece all together.

Issue 4 - In the Email body, approval work item record link should be present.
Expected Result - The AWI record should be present.
Actual Result - As of now email body approval work item record link is not present.

Issue 5 - In the email body at the end, the instructions to the respective User/ Approver should not be displayed. It is instructions so it needs to be modified with respect to AC.
Expected Result - In the email body at the end, the instructions to the respective User/ Approver should not be displayed.
Actual Result - It is instructions so it needs to be modified with respect to AC.

Issue 6 - As soon as VP BD Finance, COO, CFO, Sales VP Growth approves/ rejects the record, the status of Pro forma model record and the pro forma status field on opportunity record is being updated, ideally once all the roles including deal team members roles approve/rejects it then only status should be updated.
Expected Result - As soon as VP BD Finance, COO, CFO, SVP Growth, approves/ rejects the record, the status of Pro forma model record and the pro forma status field on opportunity record is being updated, ideally once all the roles including deal team members roles approve/rejects it then only status should be updated.
Actual Result - Status is getting updated before only as soon as VP BD Finance, COO, CFO, SVP Growth, approves/ rejects the record.

Issue 7 - The Dam Approval process is not working as no AWI records are getting created.
Expected Result - The Dam Approval process should work and AWI records should be created.
Actual Result - The Dam Approval process is not working as no AWI records are getting created.

-Issue 8 - If the user rejects the AWI Record, then its comments should be reflected under the Approval History tab, but as of now it is not being reflected.-
-Expected Result - If the user rejects the AWI Record, then its comments should be reflected under the Approval History tab.-
-Actual Result - As of now it is not being reflected, If the user rejects the AWI Record, then its comments should be reflected under the Approval History tab.-

-Issue 9 -The waiting for an approval button is not being displayed if user has already initiated Submit for Confirmation OR Submit for Contracting.-
-Expected Result - The waiting for an approval button should be displayed if user has already initiated Submit for Confirmation OR Submit for Contracting.-
-Actual Result - The waiting for an approval button should be displayed-

Issues in Pro Forma Model Approval, Rejection and Email.

 Kristin Koide PR1050558-3855, PR1050558-3879, PR1050558-3970 * Remove the New Button in Provide Network Contract Related List



* Screen Flow based on Action: Add Provider Network Contract
** Display all the Opportunity Service Records not linked to current Contract PNC
** -Query: Select Account__r.Name, Account__r.Service_Line_r.Name from Service__c where Oppotunity__c = Contract.SourceOpportunityId and Service__c.Id not in (Select Related_Opty_Service_Id__c from ProviderNetworkContract Where Contract.SourceOpportunityId = Contract.SourceOpportunityId )-
** -When Record Found:-
*** -Display records so User can select multiple PNC records-
** -When No Record Found:-
*** -No available Service Accounts.-



* When user select 1 or more service account add the Service Account to ProviderNetworkContract
** ProviderNetworkContract
*** ContractId = Current.Contract.Id
*** Related_Opty_Service_Id__c = Service__c.Id
*** Service_Account__c = Service__c.Account__c
*** {color:#bf2600}ProviderNetworkContract.HealthcarePayerNetworkId = Select Id from HealthcarePayerNetwork WHERE Name = “{color}{color:#bf2600}*Corporate Entity TBD*{color}{color:#bf2600}“{color}
*** ProviderNetworkContract.HealthcareProviderId = Select Id from HealthcareProvider Where AccountId = Service__c.Account__c)
*** StartDate = Current.Contract.StartDate
*** If Contract.EndDate != null then use PNC.EndDate = Contract.EndDate else 01/01/2099



*NOTE: Do not Show the End Date Flex Card*

Given I am a Legal Team Member within the Corporate Support Apps
When I select a *Client Contract* record
And *Status = Draft, Submitted, or Executed.*
And I am on the “Provider Network Contracts” tab
Then I have the ability to {color:#403294}‘{color}{color:#403294}*Add Provider Network Contract*{color}{color:#403294}’{color} (system will create PNC records based on Service Account record(s) that are related to the Opportunity’s Services that are in Status = Active)

* -And the Service Account record(s) have NOT already been linked to a Client Contract- {color:#403294}*-(in status = Draft, Submitted, or Executed)-*{color}{color:#403294} {color}-related to the Opportunity.- {color:#403294}And the Service Account has not already been linked to this Client Contract {color}[Service Accounts will be linked to the Client Contract via Provider Network Contract records]
* And user must select at least one Service Account to proceed.
** Display - Service Account & Type
* And if no Service Accounts are available,
** Display message: No available Service Accounts.
** Navigation option is to Cancel

And navigation options are:

* Cancel
** No records are created
** Guided experience is closed
** User is returned to the Client Contract record
* Finish
** Provider Network Contract record(s) are created
*** Record(s) are visible in the Provider Network Contract tab:
**** Related List - Fields displayed: Service Account Name, SCP Corporate Entity, Name (Provider Network Contract auto number -link), Client Termination Date
** Guided experience is closed
** User is returned to the Client Contract record

 Kristin Koide PR1050558-3856, PR1050558-3880 [https://lucid.app/lucidchart/3e2874f0-f9bf-4fa1-8510-7474dc192fb4/edit?viewport_loc=685%2C-134%2C3062%2C1543%2C0_0&invitationId=inv_16c3408e-a23f-43cc-b337-68cc38c29be9|https://lucid.app/lucidchart/3e2874f0-f9bf-4fa1-8510-7474dc192fb4/edit?viewport_loc=685%2C-134%2C3062%2C1543%2C0_0&invitationId=inv_16c3408e-a23f-43cc-b337-68cc38c29be9|smart-link]


Use a record triggered Flow for Update

WHEN Contract.RecordType = ‘Clinician’ and Contract.Status = Executed

send a platform event

Name = Clinician Onboarding

* Global Provider Id → Contract.Account.Global_Provider_ID__pc
* SSN (last 4 digits) → Contract.Account.SSN_Last_4_Digit__pc
* Zipcode → Contract.Account.PersonMailingPostalCode
* Phone → Contract.Account.Mobile
* *Fed ID* → Select FederationIdentifier from User where ContactId = Contract.Account.PersonContactId
** if blank then set as null per Aneesh 		0 Kristin Koide 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project This story updates original story [+PR1050558-3545+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3545], updates in *bold*

AC1:
Given I am an Integration User in the HC org
When a Contract record where Record Type = ‘Clinician’ AND contract Status = ‘Executed’ *AND contract’s related HPFs Provider System Status = ‘Active’* *(sent every time this criteria is met. MuleSoft will determine which Okta API to call)* *-AND ‘Federation ID’ is not populated (send once per clinician)-*
Then the system shall create the following record: (Update existing custom Platform Event)

* Platform Event, Name = Clinician Onboarding
** Global Provider Id
** SSN (last 4 digits)
** Zipcode (Mailing Address)
** Phone (Mobile)
** *Fed ID (add)*
*** *IF blank, set as null*

Assumptions:

* API handles duplicates
* -We are only creating platform event when “Federation ID” is blank.- Clinician Offboarding process should be -clearing the Federation ID on the user record and- deactivating the user in SF. [Tech - SSO Flow - SCP Offboarding - Deactivate User and Clear Fed ID|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3573]
* Okta will update the User’s ‘Federation ID’ & ‘Email'. 1/7/26 - Jose said Okta will only update 'Federation ID’, not Email
* Per [+PR1050558-3573+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3573] - SCP team will look into either automating or manually clearing the Federation ID when the Clinician deactivated in Okta.

Notes:

* {color:#0747a6}MuleSoft will determine and call either API: (see {color}[+PR1050558-3573+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3573] for assumption on update that Okta team will make on the Create API)

21/Nov/25 10:17 AM Unified Platform Phase 1 Release Done Client 360 Accepted   Low ClientMaintenance, ClinicianImpact, TADesignComplete Jasmine Moradi   PR1050558-3390, PR1050558-1682, PR1050558-2916, PR1050558-3187, PR1050558-3388, PR1050558-3389, PR1050558-4192, PR1050558-4198 saravindmaisale Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-3574

 Kristin Koide PR1050558-3858, PR1050558-3882, PR1050558-3963 Use an asynchronous path of record triggered flow for update *(Offset: 1 minute after the record updated)*

When Contract.RecordType=Clinician AND Contract.Status = Executed

For Each HPF in the Contract>PNC>HPF see if there is a forward Contract

Query1: Select AccountId, Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.Facility_Title__c*, Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__c* FROM ProviderNetworkContract WHERE ProviderNetworkContract.ContractId = Contract.Id (from Context)

Query2: Select Id, Contract__c.AccountId, Contract__c, Facility_Title__c, Process_Type__c FROM Practice_Operation_Change__c WHERE Contract__r.StartDate > Contract.StartDate (from Context) AND Contract_r.Status = “Executed” AND Contract__r.AccountId = (Query1: AccountId) AND Facility_Title__c in (Query1: Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.Facility_Title__c ) → Get the list of changes by Facility Title and Service Account

# Loop Through Query 2:
## Loop Query 1
### IF Query2.Contract__c.AccountId = Query1: AccountId AND Query2.-Contract__c.-Facility_Title__c = Query1: Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.Facility_Title__c THEN
#### Client_Change_Management__c.Contract__c = Query 2.Contract__c
#### Client_Change_Management__c.Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__c = Query 1.Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__c-r.Facility_Title__c-
#### Client_Change_Management__c.Process_Type__c = Query 2.Process_Type__c
#### Client_Change_Management__c.Status__c = New
#### Client_Change_Management__c.Practice_Operation_Change__c = Query 2.Id
# -Check if the Same Client_Change_Management__c.Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__c and Client_Change_Management__c.Contract__c is already available in Client_Change_Management__c if not (No Duplicate records key is Client_Change_Management__c.Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__c and Client_Change_Management__c.Contract__c)-
## Insert Client_Change_Management__c record(s)



The logic defined above is a pseudo code only 		0 Kristin Koide 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Check for future dated, executed *Practice Operations* *contracts*
Given I am a Clinician Contracting specialist within the Corporate Support apps
When a Clinician contract Status is set to “Executed”
Then for each of the Clinician contract’s active HPFs (HPF in status ≠ ‘Inactive’), I want the system to check for *Practice Operations* contracts meeting the following criteria:

* Future ‘Contract Start Date' & Status = ‘Executed’

And if:

* Match is found proceed to AC2
* Match is NOT found do nothing

AC2: Check for matching *Practice Operation Change* record
Given a Clinician contract Status is set to “Executed”
When a matching Practice Operations contract is found in AC1
Then the system shall check for a matching *Practice Operation Change* record based on “Facility Title” of the Healthcare Practitioner Facility (HPF) record
And if:

* Match is found proceed to AC3
* Match is NOT found do nothing

AC3: Create *Client Change Management* record
Given a Clinician contract Status is set to “Executed”
When a matching Practice Operations contract is found in AC1
And a matching *Practice Operation Change* record if found in AC2
Then the system shall create *Client Change Management* record 		21/Nov/25 10:17 AM   4 15/Oct/25 7:20 PM SCP Sprint 12 11/Nov/25 6:04 AM 1|i099yr:zzuzftwzzlzzzczi 29/Jan/26 9:04 PM As a Clinician Contracting Specialist, when a Clinician contract is Executed and there is a future dated Practice Operations contract in Status = ‘Executed’, I want the system to automatically create a *Client Change Management* record, so that additional automation based on the 'Process Type' (Manual, Agent, Automation) can occur. 10/Nov/25

Client Change Management - Clinician Contract Executed between PO Contracts

 0 Kristin Koide 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Reactivate Experience Cloud User in Salesforce
Given I am a Clinician (prospect) that was an Active Clinician that left SCP
And I have a Person Account with Status = Inactive & 'Global Provider ID' populated
And I have a inactive Experience Cloud User record with ‘Federation ID’ NOT populated (blank)
When I reapply/try to get rehired
And either:

* ATS Applicant’s field ATS Status = “*Awaiting Intake Form”* [For more details see original story - [+PR1050558-1789+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1789]]
** With appropriate Person Account (Clinician record) linked
-OR-
* -Specific User select the “*Enable Customer User*” from the Person Account (Clinician record) [For more details see original story -- [+-PR1050558-811-+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-811]-]- - This option is just for new users, not to enable a deactivated user.

Then the system shall reactivate the previous Experience Cloud User record

* Update the User’s ‘Email’ to their personal email on their Person Account

And send the {color:#0747a6}Welcome Email (see story for template {color} [+PR1050558-3013+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3013]) -Reset Password email- (User can reset their password) {color:#0747a6}[11/18: updated AC. Per solution design, we were able to reuse the Welcome Email template, we did NOT need to create a new Reset Password Email Template){color}

And from Portal Login, the user will input their email (personal)
And system directs them to login to HC Portal via Username and Password (story [+PR1050558-3546+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3546])

*-Reset Password Email Template:-*
-Dear {!User.FirstName},-

-We received a request to reset your password for {!Community.Name}. To set a new password, click the link below. The link expires after 24 hours.-

-{!Community.Url}-

-Thank you,-
-{!Organization.Name}-

AC4: Given I am an Integration User in the HC org (updates story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614]+,+ updates in {color:#bf2600}red{color})
When a Contract record where Record Type = ‘Clinician’ AND contract Status = ‘Executed’ AND contract’s related HPFs Provider System Status = ‘Active’ {color:#bf2600}*or ‘Candidate’*{color} (sent every time this criteria is met. MuleSoft will determine which Okta API to call)
Then the system shall create the following record: (Update existing custom Platform Event)

* Platform Event, Name = Clinician Onboarding
** Global Provider Id
** SSN (last 4 digits)
** Zipcode (Mailing Address)
** Phone (Mobile)
** Fed ID (add)
*** IF blank, set as null

– For AC 2 &3 can be tested in an environment where Okta is set up. No work in SF, just needs to be tested in QA. Need to coordinate with the Okta team - Sravan Kumar Naladala – NOTE: testing ACs split to story [+PR1050558-4191+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4191]

-AC2: Remove SSO Permission-
-Given I am a Clinician that left SCP-
-When Okta deactivated the Experience Cloud User-
-Then Okta shall also ensure that the SSO Permission set has been removed-

-AC3: Add SSO Permission-
-Given I am a Clinician (Active) that has an executed contract and contract’s related HPF(s) Provider System Status = ‘Active’- {color:#bf2600}*-or ‘Candidate’-*{color} -(part of story- [+-PR1050558-3614-+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614]+-,-+ -update in- {color:#bf2600}-red-{color} -based on AC4)-
-When Okta has update the ‘Federation ID’ & ‘Email’ fields on the Experience Cloud User-
-Then Okta shall ensure that the SSO Permission set has been added-
-And from Portal Login, the user will input their email-
-And system directs to the SSO login (story- [+-PR1050558-3546-+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3546]-)-

Assumptions:

# Upon initial User Deactivation:
## Okta will deactivate the Okta User
## Okta will deactivate the Experience Cloud User & remove the access.
## The SCP Team has either automatically or manually cleared the Experience Cloud User’s ‘Federation ID’ field for Phase 1.
# Upon reapply/rehire:
## The Clinician has one person account linked to one user record
# Upon Clinician contract Executed: (see story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614])
## The SCP/Okta Team has updated the Create API to reactivate the Okta User via the Global Provider ID.
### If there is a Global Provider ID, then Okta will send the appropriate activation email.
### If there is NO Global Provider ID, then Okta will create the new user and appropriate email is sent
## Okta activates Okta User
## Okta updates the Experience Cloud User’s ‘Federation ID’ & ‘Email’

----

REFINEMENT NOTES

{color:#bf2600}Question:{color}

# {color:#bf2600}Jose - As part of AC3 which is related to story {color}[https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614|smart-link]{color:#bf2600}, as part of our new criteria trigger the platform event, in addition to the Clinician’s Contract Status = Executed, we are also checking if contracts {color}{color:#bf2600}*related HPF(s) ‘Provider System Status’ field to equal ‘Active’. Are we sure that we want to include this new criteria?*{color}{color:#bf2600} {color}{color:#0747a6}*11/11, we will leave this criteria on story 3614. Jose will check with business if this criteria needs to be adjusted and if so will adjust ‘Provider System Status’ formula field to make the status = “Active” at the right time.*{color}{color:#0747a6} {color}

{color:#bf2600}There is current automation the HPF ‘Status’ is set to ‘Active’, when the ‘First Clinical Shift’ is populated. However, it is towards the end of the onboarding process. It seems a bit late in the process to wait for the 'Provider System Status’ field to equal ‘Active’ to provision Okta.{color}

Screenshot showing where Contract Status = Executed and where the HPF *Status* = Active.

Also the E2E says Okta SSO is provisioned when Contract Status = Executed (screenshot below is a close up shot of the first green box).
